top_signals.title: Japan’s Stocks Set to Sink on Tech Selloff, Middle East Worries
top_signals.summary: Japanese stocks are set to fall, led by tech shares, after strong US jobs data fueled expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer, sparking a selloff in AI-related shares on Wall Street.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Shipping: Will El Niño Further Disrupt Cargo Flows?
top_signals.summary: This year’s El Niño could bring more cargo flow shifts. In a recent weekly report, shipbroker Intermodal said that “El Niño is again becoming a factor for 2026, and its freight impact is likely to be felt mainly through Asian power demand, crop risk and trade-flow shifts. The latest probability set points to El Niño ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Defence spending plan delay undermines UK credibility, MPs say
top_signals.summary: The delay in publishing Keir Starmer’s long-awaited plan on defence spending has undermined the UK’s credibility and left the country less safe, a parliamentary committee has warned. In a new report critisising the long delay in publishing Starmer’s Defence Investment Plan (DIP), the Public Accounts Committee said this “has undermined the UK government’s credibility with [...]

top_signals.title: BoC expected to hold rates through 2026, look past temporary inflation pressures - Reuters
top_signals.summary: BoC expected to hold rates through 2026, look past temporary inflation pressures  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Britain's Net Zero Policy Is Killing Its Chemical Industry
top_signals.summary: From ammonia to plastics, there can be no modern economy without a functioning chemicals industry, and Britain's is in peril, writes Sharon Todd The recent announcement of a £350m Critical Chemicals Resilience Fund from Chancellor Rachel Reeves is welcome but it is also an acknowledgement of an uncomfortable truth: the UK's chemicals sector is in trouble. Up and down the country energy intensive manufacturing is faltering. The Chancellor has also announced £120m for the ceramics sector, providing relief to another struggling sector.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
